phase_inference_basis: Pre-treatment desperation framing.
raw_excerpt: .for me, it was kind of it felt like kind of like the last chance saloon really to kind of do something.
notes: Anchors person_profile.expectancy_valence='negative' (last-resort framing).

phase_inference_basis: Anticipation across multiple sessions; describes setting-derived safety.
raw_excerpt: I had no idea what would happen. but then after that, the second and third I sort of went into the sessions with more intent to explore and to get answers from certain things. it was nice to just do that and know that you're in a safe environment and you can come out the other side. It filled you with confidence and you know there's no paranoia.
notes: setting_profile.perceived_safety='high'; expectancy improves across sessions (relevant for repeated-infusion modeling).

phase_inference_basis: Anticipatory fear of intensity, contrasted with reassurance from setting.
raw_excerpt: I just thought, right ‘How extreme is this experience going to be? Is it going to be a ride from hell or something like that?” And it absolutely wasn't and that's down to the laboratory type conditions, the controlled environment
notes: Demonstrates setting buffering pre-session fear. Useful for setting_profile interaction effects.

phase_inference_basis: Sustained calmness with preserved self-awareness — peak with intact meta-awareness.
raw_excerpt: It was peaceful. It was calming and I just sat back and. you know, I didn't lose sense of who I was. I always knew who I was, and I had confidence that this would be temporary.
notes: Anchor for 'gentle peak' archetype: dissociation low, valence positive, identity intact.

phase_inference_basis: Reinforcing-effect quote — characterizes the acute experience's positive valence.
raw_excerpt: I wanted this to carry on every week for the rest of my life. I wanted this experience in this, you know, sort of clinical place, I would still be coming now you know what I mean?
notes: Reinforcement signal — informs simulator's model of post-session craving / loss-on-cessation.

phase_inference_basis: Acute fear with somatic threat (breathing).
raw_excerpt: I knew where I was, but I just, it wasn't a pleasant experience like the first one and then the end part, the second one. It was, it was scary. And to the point of when you're on it, you barely breathe, you've quite shallow breathing.
notes: Anchor for 'fear-dominant peak'. medical_anxiety=true (breath/somatic threat).

phase_inference_basis: Co-occurring comfort + paranoia, with time-distortion implied by 'rest of my life'.
raw_excerpt: So it was a sort of mixture of extreme comfort for want of a better word with a sort of paranoia where one's brain is saying, if you guys in the room will leave the room, I'm stuck here for the rest of my life, sort of thing.
notes: Demonstrates fear and emotional_valence are independent — comfort + fear coexist.

phase_inference_basis: Motor paralysis is a peak-phase phenomenon.
raw_excerpt: It's almost like a state of paralysis, where you do receive visual and sound information. But I don't know how much sense I was making to the people in the room around me. It feels like when you try and run in a dream, but your legs are move fast enough. In fact, exactly like that.
notes: loss_of_agency dominant; preserved sensory channels but blocked motor execution.

phase_inference_basis: Somatic-distress peak with movement-triggered nausea.
raw_excerpt: I was I didn't feel particularly brilliant, to be honest. And then I started moving and just was aware I was going to be sick. And then I was quite violently sick. So that was my experience.
notes: Pure somatic-aversion record, no dissociative/psychedelic content. Anchor for non-mystical adverse profile.

phase_inference_basis: 'Outworldly' / 'out of this world' — clear peak/k-hole-adjacent dissociative content.
raw_excerpt: Well, that infusion took me somewhere that was out outworldly, was out of this world. It was not—it was not within this world.
notes: Otherworldly framing implies cosmic_scale + identity_shift even without explicit ego-dissolution language.

phase_inference_basis: Ego dissolution + cosmic unity — classic peak phenomenology.
raw_excerpt: The experience was genuinely remarkable in terms of the both the visual effects and…. the sort of removal of ego that accompanied that. I felt that I was one with the whole universe and it sounds hippy dippy but that's how I felt. I think I related to this sort of ego changing, the size of the ego in me as well as a sort of physical sensation.
notes: Anchor for 'mystical-positive' archetype: meaningfulness=1.0, fear=0, valence=+0.75.

phase_inference_basis: Cross-session evolution — visit-to-visit comparison with first visit at peak intensity.
raw_excerpt: So, the first one was very trippy and like going into another dimension. And then the second one, because I wanted to go into that dimension, I think I was trying to go there and then I got a bit sort of like, oh, I can hear voices. This is putting me off. And then the third one. I wasn't well at all after that. As I said I couldn't come around from it.
source_theme_label: Otherworldly — Changes in experiences over time
notes: Cross-session trajectory variation. Useful for temporal-dynamics modeling (multiple infusions → divergent profiles).

phase_inference_basis: Visual change tied to acute experience.
raw_excerpt: I remember looking at the light above my head in the window and sort of blinking and as I blinked the of the colours changed. Even though. the picture in my mind, the light in the window was the same.
notes: Visual distortion isolated from dissociative content — single-dimension anchor.

phase_inference_basis: Auditory transformation of music — acute peak with intact recognition.
raw_excerpt: .there were pieces that I was familiar with, but they were just, they were coming—It's like different aspects that made up the piece were coming through a different speeds or different pitches. They were just sounding like unique. … I could still hear them as piano pieces but they weren't the pieces that I knew.
notes: Pure auditory_change anchor; recognition intact.

phase_inference_basis: Complex visual-conceptual hallucination zooming to cosmic scale.
raw_excerpt: I saw.this very strong vision of the synapse travelling across the neural pathway, then me typing into my phone, him getting the message him talking to someone else, and like this chain of communication, essentially that got bigger and bigger and bigger and bigger and bigger, to the point where it zoomed right out and you're seeing the earth hanging there in space along with say 20, 25, 30 other inhabited planets all there in the cosmos.
notes: visual_imagery=1.0; cosmic_scale + tunnel_void_space_imagery both true. Conceptual-narrative-rich hallucination, distinct from purely perceptual.

phase_inference_basis: Hallucinated character transformation of staff — peak visual hallucination.
raw_excerpt: So, everybody turned into Alice in the Wonderland for me. … And then the other one I can't remember her name, she is wonderful, but she was the Cheshire cat. So, when she walked in, she had this massive grin.
notes: Person-perception hallucination; entity_or_presence=true (staff transformed into characters).

phase_inference_basis: Within-peak fluctuation between geometric and chromatic visual modes.
raw_excerpt: Sometimes it would become very linear, everything will be at right angles and lines and squares. And other times it would be just like a blur it would just be colours. And it would constantly change and evolve, what I was seeing would evolve and what I was hearing would evolve as well.
notes: Within-phase fluctuation evidence. Useful for state-vector instability modeling at peak.

phase_inference_basis: Oneness/finality content — peak mystical phenomenology.
raw_excerpt: It was a sense of completeness sense of, I suppose in a way finality, a source of finish. But also, a sense of enormous growth and a feeling of oneness with other entities, other living beings in particular, but also the world and universe as a whole.
notes: death_rebirth_theme=true on the basis of 'finality / source of finish + enormous growth' juxtaposition.

phase_inference_basis: Insight content during the acute experience.
raw_excerpt: I think the first two like, they sort of left me… like they answered a lot of questions. You know, thinking about my children and I have a stepdaughter and… It was things about that. And about, you know what I should be doing and how I should be, you know, I don't know it seemed to be like all the things that are really heavy on my mind and that I stress about whatever it was sort of going through those things.just realising how little importance some things had or were.
source_theme_label: Meaningful, spiritual, mystical — Epiphanies and Enlightenment
notes: Pure insight=1.0 anchor with low dissociation — counter to expectation that insight requires deep dissociation.

phase_inference_basis: Time-perception transformation explicitly described.
raw_excerpt: .while we're on the subject of time. I mean, that just goes bananas and I love it. It's almost like. this afternoon, this evening, the way we think of that—That just blows apart and doesn't exist anymore.
source_theme_label: Meaningful, spiritual, mystical — Transcendence of time
notes: Cleanest single-dimension anchor (time_distortion=1.0). Positive valence — time loss experienced as wonder.

phase_inference_basis: Religious vision with explicit entity encounter.
raw_excerpt: I was lying there, and there was this, there was this like a cacophony coming from the hallway, everyone was going like, like he's coming he's coming! And I was like what's going on, you know, like thinking this shouldn't be happening in the hospital, I thought this is a peaceful place and everyone's like rushing out into the hallway to see what's happening. And it's this cartoon very simply drawn constructed of neon light rendering of God, essentially is walking down the corridor.and he came up to me and he was like.you can ask me two questions.
source_theme_label: Meaningful, spiritual, mystical — Hallucinations or visions
notes: entity_or_presence=true. Highest visual_imagery + meaningfulness combo. Religious framing in a non-religious setting.

phase_inference_basis: Spiritual-content peak with universal-connectedness theme.
raw_excerpt: …we are all connected and there is this connection between all beings, people and things to again bring us out of this kind of prison of addiction. The transpersonal effects of the drug bring us out of ourselves and put the problems into perspective.
notes: Connectedness + identity dissolution oriented toward addiction-recovery framing.

phase_inference_basis: Paranoid ideation about the trial during acute experience.
raw_excerpt: I can remember thinking, feeling very paranoid that the whole trial was kind of a fake and it was like trying to get me into this situation. And that I was actually going to be in a situation for the rest of my life.
source_theme_label: Negative experiences — Paranoia about the trial
notes: Same participant as P04_peak_01 (calmness) — same person can have qualitatively opposite peaks across infusions. Argues for stochastic transition model rather than person-deterministic.

phase_inference_basis: Post-trial perspective reflection.
raw_excerpt: It helped family wise, relationship wise in every, every single avenue of my life. It's changed it.doing the ketamine and seeing this other dimension enforced my belief of another life and I now live every single day to the max. When I go for a walk, I'm very observant of my world around me. I take pleasures in life rather than pleasures of.drink. So.it's still with me and I hope it'll stay with me for forever.
notes: Same participant as P03_peak_01 (fear-dominant) — fear-dominant peak does not preclude positive afterglow.

phase_inference_basis: Retrospective transformational claim.
raw_excerpt: In a non-cheesy way, it actually probably changed my life around and kept me alive.
notes: Important correction from pilot v1: this is the actual P12 quote. 'This is something that changed my life' (paper title) is a paraphrase, not a verbatim quote.

phase_inference_basis: Behavioral change post-trial regarding alcohol.
raw_excerpt: I think before the trial all my life was sort of focused around alcohol. I was either drinking it at home or selling it to students or working in an event where there was alcohol, the alcohol was a focus of it. So it was sort of everything and then afterwards, it just sort of stopped. I enjoy a drink every now and then, but under much safer ways really.So it just made me realise I don't need to sort of drink to excess because there's nothing else to do. I can just do other things.and that alcohol isn't everything.
notes: Behavioral-outcome afterglow record. Useful for post-session integration modeling.

phase_inference_basis: Post-trial perspective leveraging the experience as a reference point.
raw_excerpt: So moving onwards in life, every time I get something that's quite testing or you know a problem, I just say, 'well, a couple of months ago, I was just white…. So how bad can this be?' … At least I'm not white, you know, at least I've got a body, at least I'm alive kind of thing.
notes: death_rebirth_theme=true: 'I was just white...at least I've got a body, at least I'm alive' — symbolic dying/returning frame.

phase_inference_basis: Identity-change reflection post-process.
raw_excerpt: …My persona was a jacket that I could take off and hang up at the door and do the work. And then put that jacket back on. But the jacket would be a slightly different colour and then by the end of the process, it was a different jacket.
notes: Metaphorical identity_shift; valence positive but moderate (incremental change framing).

phase_inference_basis: Cross-session post-experience reflection on lasting state.
raw_excerpt: I just found I left every session, kind of, you know, feeling happy. Feeling, you know… feeling lighter… Feeling like clearer thoughts.
notes: Anchor for 'gentle afterglow': cognitive_coherence + narrative_coherence both 0.75, valence positive, no dissociative residue.

phase_inference_basis: Reflective synthesis on full trial experience.
raw_excerpt: So, I think that as a package, I hit the golden button, didn't I? …. Not only did I get a life changing and mind-altering experience, but then the therapist did plug some new thoughts to me that made me think differently. I feel that it is really important that when you are split open, you know, in such an intense and life changing way that you are given new thoughts and you know that someone gives you something to refill that, so you do change stuff.
notes: 'Split open' / 'refill' implies death_rebirth_theme adjacent metaphor — kept narrow as identity_shift only.
